about.purpose: This application provides real-time flight delay estimations by combining historical operational flight data with live meteorology reports. It assists dispatchers, pilots, and movement control teams in anticipating scheduling issues.
about.importance: Flight delays propagate through airline networks, inflating operational costs and disrupting passenger plans. Predicting delays beforehand helps in proactive re-routing, fuel optimization, and gate scheduling.
about.ml_usage: By training machine learning classifiers on hundreds of thousands of past flights and corresponding hourly local weather conditions, the system learns complex, non-linear relationships that trigger operational holds.
data_sources: Flight operational logs (movement types, airline codes, schedules)
Open-Meteo API hourly historical and real-time weather datasets

classification.on_time: The flight is expected to depart or arrive within 30 minutes of its scheduled time.
classification.delay: The flight is expected to deviate from its scheduled time by 30 minutes or more.
classification.definition: A flight is officially classified as 'Delayed' if its Actual Time of Departure (ATD) is 30 minutes or more after its Scheduled Time of Departure (STD).

weather_parameters.name: Temperature
weather_parameters.description: The ambient atmospheric temperature measured 2 meters above ground level.
weather_parameters.impact: High heat reduces air density, decreasing lift and engine thrust. This forces aircraft weight restrictions, potentially causing cargo-loading delays.

weather_parameters.name: Visibility
weather_parameters.description: The horizontal distance at which objects can be clearly seen and identified by pilots or controllers.
weather_parameters.impact: Low visibility below airport minimums halts visual flight operations, forcing instrument approaches with wider separations, directly causing backlogs.

weather_parameters.name: Wind Speed
weather_parameters.description: The rate of air movement measured horizontally at 10 meters and 100 meters above the runway.
weather_parameters.impact: High wind speeds, especially crosswinds exceeding aircraft structural limits, suspend runway operations or force aircraft to hold.

weather_parameters.name: Wind Direction
weather_parameters.description: The compass heading from which the wind originates (0° = North, 180° = South).
weather_parameters.impact: Crucial for runway configuration. Tailwind components force changes in active runway headings, delaying taxi and departure schedules.

weather_parameters.name: Precipitation
weather_parameters.description: The depth of liquid water (rain) falling on the ground over a specific hour.
weather_parameters.impact: Heavy rain degrades visibility, causes water accumulation on runway surfaces (hydroplaning risk), and interrupts ground handling operations.

weather_parameters.name: Humidity
weather_parameters.description: The amount of water vapor present in the air relative to the maximum capacity at that temperature.
weather_parameters.impact: High relative humidity combined with temperature shifts leads to early fog formation, severely reducing runway visual range.

weather_parameters.name: Surface Pressure
weather_parameters.description: The atmospheric pressure measured directly at the airport surface level.
weather_parameters.impact: Low pressure systems represent storms, unstable weather, and turbulence, while high pressure systems generally represent stable conditions.

weather_parameters.name: Cloud Cover
weather_parameters.description: The fraction of the sky obscured by clouds, categorized by Low, Mid, and High altitude cover.
weather_parameters.impact: Low-level clouds block instrument approaches and glide paths, whereas high-level clouds have negligible impact on ground operations.

weather_parameters.name: Weather Code
weather_parameters.description: World Meteorological Organization numeric code representing specific weather descriptors (e.g. thunderstorm, drizzle, fog).
weather_parameters.impact: Instantly categorizes general severity. Active thunderstorms (Codes 95-99) or heavy freezing drizzle suspend all ground actions.

visibility.description: Severe delays. Approaching runway shutdown limits.

wind_speed.description: Crosswind alerts. Ground crew holds possible.
Severe crosswinds. Expect diversions or suspensions.

prediction_guide.on_time_meaning: The model calculates a low probability of departure delay. Under the selected parameters, normal timelines are highly likely.
prediction_guide.delay_meaning: The model calculates a high probability of a departure delay exceeding 30 minutes. Operational adjustments are recommended.
prediction_guide.confidence_expl: The probability percentage indicates the model's mathematical confidence in the predicted class. For example, a 92% delay probability means the model is extremely certain that operational parameters align with past historical delay patterns.

ml_models.name: Decision Tree
advantages: Highly intuitive rules that can be visualized as a flowchart
Directly maps decisions based on sequential feature splitting

ml_models.name: XGBoost
advantages: Ensemble boosting algorithm creating hundreds of trees sequentially
Highest predictive accuracy and robust generalization on unseen patterns

feature_importance.intro: Not every atmospheric or operational metric influences flight schedules equally. The machine learning model weighs features based on historical correlations.

top_features.impact: Highest impact. Poor visibility blocks landings and departures completely.
High impact. Wet runways increase braking distances and slow down ground handling.
Moderate impact. Affects structural crosswind safety margins.
Slight impact. Affects engine thrust performance on hot days.

faq.question: What is classified as a flight delay in this system?
faq.answer: A flight delay is defined as any departure occurring 30 minutes or more after its scheduled departure time (STD).

faq.question: Why can weather cause flight delays?
faq.answer: Severe weather conditions such as heavy rainfall, low visibility, and high winds jeopardize flight safety. Air Traffic Control (ATC) increases spacing between flights, slowing down operations, while ground crews may halt ramp actions.

faq.question: Why doesn't every rainy day cause delays?
faq.answer: Light rain or rain with excellent visibility and calm winds is easily manageable. Delays usually happen when rain is heavy, runway surfaces accumulate water, visibility drops below landing minimums, or strong winds occur simultaneously.

faq.question: Why is the prediction output sometimes different from reality?
faq.answer: Machine learning models predict based on historical statistics and weather forecasts. They cannot anticipate unpredictable events such as unscheduled maintenance, late-arriving crew, medical emergencies, or sudden ATC issues.

faq.question: How accurate is the prediction model?
faq.answer: The tuned XGBoost model achieves an accuracy of approximately 85% to 90% on testing datasets. This accuracy may vary depending on the precision of incoming weather forecasts.

faq.question: Can administrators retrain the model?
faq.answer: Yes. Admin users can upload new historical logs (operational flight logs and local METAR weather indices) to trigger a automated pipeline run that retrains and deploys updated model files.

faq.question: How often should the model be retrained?
faq.answer: Retraining is typically performed quarterly or during season changes (e.g. wet season vs dry season) to capture shifting monsoon wind patterns and changes in baseline schedules.

workflow.name: Flight Data
workflow.desc: Input scheduled airline, flight operation type, hour, and date info.

workflow.name: Weather Data
workflow.desc: Fetch live ambient weather metrics (wind, rain, clouds, visibility) from forecast models.

workflow.name: Preprocessing
workflow.desc: Calculate derived ratios (e.g. wind gust ratios, rain-cloud indices) to align features.

workflow.name: ML Model
workflow.desc: Run calculations through the trained XGBoost or Decision Tree classifiers.

workflow.name: Prediction Result
workflow.desc: Evaluate outcomes, mapping outputs to On-Time or Delay with confidence probabilities.

workflow.name: Dashboard
workflow.desc: Render formatted summaries, color badges, and warning notes on the screen.

glossary.definition: The airport authority unit responsible for regulating runway gate parking, pushback clearances, and aircraft taxi operations on the apron.
The predicted timestamp at which an aircraft will land on the runway.
The predicted timestamp at which an aircraft will begin its pushback from the parking bay.
The official, timetabled departure time published by the airline.
The exact timestamp when an aircraft breaks contact with the gate or starts taxiing for takeoff.
A 3-letter code designating airports worldwide (e.g., KNO for Kualanamu, CGK for Soekarno-Hatta) or a 2-letter code for airlines (e.g., GA for Garuda).
A 4-letter airport indicator (e.g. WIMM for Kualanamu) or 3-letter airline indicator (e.g. GIA for Garuda) used by pilots and air traffic control.
A defined rectangular area on a land aerodrome prepared for the landing and takeoff of aircraft.
A paved path at an airport connecting runways with aprons, hangars, and terminals, used for aircraft taxiing.
Also called the ramp, this is the area where aircraft are parked, loaded, unloaded, refueled, or boarded.
An operational procedure where an aircraft is pushed backward away from an airport gate by external tug power.
Services provided to aircraft while parked at terminal gates, including baggage handling, refueling, catering, and cabin cleaning.
The branch of science concerned with the processes and phenomena of the atmosphere, especially as a means of forecasting the weather.
A subfield of artificial intelligence focused on building algorithms that learn patterns from datasets to make automated decisions.
A supervised machine learning model that maps parameters to predictions using hierarchical logical branching rules.
An optimized gradient boosting machine learning library that combines hundreds of simple decision trees to yield high performance.
Receiver Operating Characteristic - Area Under Curve. A statistical metric used to measure classification model discriminative performance.
Evaluation metrics: Precision measures positive predictive accuracy, Recall measures detection rate, and F1 Score combines both into a single harmonic mean.
A table layout used to visualize the performance of a classification algorithm by comparing actual values with predicted outcomes.
